title: Minimum Window Substring
description: Given two strings s and t, return the minimum window in s which will contain all the characters in t. If there is no such window in s that covers all characters in t, return an empty string. Note that if there is such a window, it is guaranteed that there will always be only one unique minimum window.

examples.explanation: The substring "BANC" contains all the characters of "ABC" and is the smallest such substring.
The entire string "a" is the minimum window containing the character "a".
There is no window in s that contains all characters of t since t requires two 'a's and s only has one.

constraints: All characters in s and t are English letters.
Characters in t may not be unique.
hints: Consider using a sliding window to expand and contract the potential substrings.
Maintain a count of characters from t that need to be included in the current window.
Use two pointers to mark the start and end of your window.
Think about how to efficiently check if the current window contains all characters from t.
